lyrics:
Raven Dark was of the One
She created herself to know the Other

She dreamed dimensions into being
so the Other could exist
She cried and sparked vibration
into the silence
She forged the dust
with the force of her wings

And she waited

I see... (Raven Dark)
I hear... (Raven Dark)
I feel... (Raven Dark)

We sing... (Raven Dark)
We call... (Raven Dark)
We love... (Raven Dark)

Raven Dark grew hungry
for experience
She plucked a feather from her tail
and with her oldest voice
sang life into the dust
Raven Dark longed
for her song to be heard
She brought forth the first people
so they could listen and love

And she guides them still

I see... (Raven Dark)
I hear... (Raven Dark)
I feel... (Raven Dark)

We sing... (Raven Dark)
We call... (Raven Dark)
We love... (Raven Dark)

I see... (Raven Dark)
I hear... (Raven Dark)
I feel... (Raven Dark)

We sing... (Raven Dark)
We call... (Raven Dark)
We love... (Raven Dark)

And she guides
And she waits
persona:
## STAB

STAB isn’t just a name-it’s a statement. A sonic force of nature, she channels the electric pulse of '80s pop-rock, fusing jangly guitars, driving basslines, and tight harmonies into anthems that hit hard and leave a mark. Bold, confident, and unapologetically fierce, STAB delivers high-energy hooks with a rebellious edge, embracing the raw, unfiltered spirit of rock.

Her music is a love letter to the golden age of girl bands, blending the infectious melodies of The Bangles with the gritty attitude of The Runaways. Whether she’s tearing through anthemic choruses, throwing down sharp riffs, or tapping into her softer, more introspective side, STAB’s voice is unmistakable-powerful yet effortlessly cool.

A signature artist under Maricopa Records, STAB thrives in the space where analog warmth meets AI-driven creativity, pushing boundaries while staying true to the timeless essence of rock 'n' roll. She’s not here to follow trends-she’s here to carve her own path, one catchy, defiant, and undeniably addictive track at a time.

If you’re looking for music that hits like a lightning bolt and stays in your head for days-STAB is already in your veins.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/7zZ7MxU20RZAw2E1w4EsaE)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/stab/1495050512)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCQkfMbYRs06q_SFW5PPSvWw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/STAB.jpg)
## Releases

impression.text: This track creates a dark and mystical atmosphere, opening with a spacious, ethereal texture of spoken word and ambient synths before exploding into a high-energy, driving industrial rock feel. The production is modern and powerful, skillfully balancing wide, atmospheric synth layers with a dense, focused core of punchy drums, heavy guitars, and a clear, soaring female vocal. The most defining quality is the stark dynamic contrast, perfectly exemplified by the abrupt transition at 0:26 where the song’s initial calm is shattered by the full band’s powerful entry. A highly technical and melodic guitar solo section later provides a moment of impressive instrumental focus.

review.review_text: STAB reaches for something genuinely ambitious here, building a creation myth around a raven goddess in G minor at a mid-tempo 96 BPM, and the production earns the mythology: that cold-open of ambient synths and spoken word shatters into full-band impact around the 26-second mark with real conviction, the kind of dynamic gut-punch that Evanescence used to pull off on their best days. The call-and-response chorus, 'I see / I hear / I feel' answered by the chanted name, locks in a ritualistic groove that grows with each pass. The guitar solo section gives the track its beating heart, and the lyric 'she plucked a feather from her tail / and with her oldest voice / sang life into the dust' is quietly beautiful. A highlight, and proof STAB has more range than the pure rock-anthem mode suggests.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'she plucked a feather from her tail / and with her oldest voice / sang life into the dust'
